free_text: Nucleosynthesis of Li,Be and B-contributions from the
The natural O(2) target gas. Typical O(2) gas pressures
a  time-of-flight telescope was used which consisted of
timing signals provided by a channel-plate fast-timing
device (CPFTD) and a 75 mum delta-E detector.
A 700 mum E detector was placed behind the 75 mum
detector and was used for particle identification.
All energy detectors were totally depleted
For the 90-MeV run,the TOF measurements were performed
using rf timing techniques in conjunction with a 15 mum
delta-E and 75 mum E detector telescope.
The fragments with energies greater 0.1-0.2 MeV/nucleon
could be detected. Timing signals from the CPFTD were
derived from secondary electrons emitted when the
fragment of interest passed through a 30 mug/cm**2
carbon foil. The electrons were accelerated through a
potential of 1200 V onto two microchannel plates in
series,which provided a net gain of 10E-5 - 10E-6. The
charge was collected on an anode cone,amplified by a
The differential cross section in the unobserved region
was assumed to be constant and identical to that at
the maximum in DDX; one-half the cross section
evaluated in this region was then added to the measured
yield. An error identical to this amount was added in
quadrature to the other sources of error in determining
